scope_boundary: deterministic resource-budget, generation-mode, budget-ledger, capacity-smoothing toy-trace, workflow-trace, local command-replay, local five-sample workload-quality route selection, CI publication-metadata, and simulation-contract record accounting only; no budget scheduler, review-queue optimizer, production scheduler log, live or external workload-quality review, stable speedup result, load-stability experiment, KV-cache memory measurement, cost-quality economics result, verification-tax optimization, runtime budget enforcement, serving benchmark, simulation run, physical-computation audit, fidelity calibration, or benchmark-transfer review
evidence_roles: simulation_contract_record fixture validates record shape only
SimulationFidelity Lean hooks are finite record-level predicates only
transition_reason: The repository now has resource-budget schema validation, generation-mode fixtures that require budget refs, useful-solution-per-second accounting, quality metrics, residual metrics, fallback behavior, and no-promotion boundaries, a deterministic budget-ledger harness for dispatch, high-risk escalation, protected overhead, displaced-cost residualization, scarce review-capacity deferral, and expected-invalid shortcut rejection, plus a capacity-smoothing toy harness for bounded regeneration arithmetic, priority deferral, scope reduction, and overload rejection. The later workflow trace adds three deterministic selected routes with selected-route cost recomputation, protected high-risk-first scheduling, displaced-cost residual ownership, latency-only rejection, physical-feasibility-overclaim rejection, and Lean summary alignment. The live replay probe replays five local Resource Economics validators and records command-output digests plus tracked artifact hashes. The workload-quality probe records a local five-sample scoped route choice: the Resource workflow trace validator is selected over the broader Resource live-probe baseline by median elapsed time while a cheaper no-op success-text route is rejected for not running the required validator. The CI cost profile records recent GitHub Pages publication run durations, one generated-scaffold failure, and one repair run as publication-pipeline metadata. These artifacts improve record discipline, replay discipline, local route-selection discipline, and local operations accounting for future budget tests, but they do not implement a budget scheduler, optimize a review queue, provide production scheduler logs, review live or external workload quality, prove stable speedup, measure real load stability, reproduce KV-cache memory behavior, quantify verification-tax optimization, or establish an economic outcome. The folded simulation-fidelity lane adds a Simulation Contract Record fixture and finite Lean predicates for scope/fidelity/resource completeness and declared-support limits, but those artifacts remain record discipline only and do not validate simulator adequacy, physical feasibility, or transfer.
required_artifacts: budget allocator or scheduler run with task, route, capacity, and protected-gate records
load-stability scenario with burst, regeneration, deferral, and failure accounting
review-capacity allocation trace with actual high-risk and low-risk queue behavior
external or live workload-facing trace with real workload quality review beyond the local repository task
production scheduler logs beyond repository CI metadata
risk-adjusted verification-tax experiment with baseline and negative control
KV-cache or serving-memory measurement tied to resource-budget records
cost-quality tradeoff run with residual and human-review accounting
independent review of source interpretation adequacy
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
simulation-transfer review with declared scope, fidelity, resource bill, omissions, instrumentation effects, transfer decision, and negative cases
physical-computation audit tied to an actual simulation claim
fidelity calibration benchmark with baseline and no-transfer cases
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_generation_mode_baselines.py && python3 scripts/validate_resource_budget_ledgers.py && python3 scripts/validate_capacity_smoothing.py && python3 scripts/validate_resource_workflow_trace.py && python3 scripts/validate_resource_live_probe.py && python3 scripts/validate_resource_workload_quality_probe.py && python3 scripts/validate_resource_ci_cost_profile.py && python3 scripts/validate_evidence_transitions.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: resource-budget fixtures are deterministic accounting fixtures
budget-ledger fixtures are deterministic decision fixtures
capacity-smoothing fixtures are deterministic toy traces
workflow-trace fixtures are deterministic public-safe accounting traces
local replay probes are repository command replays only
workload-quality probes are local five-sample repository task measurements only
CI cost profiles are publication-pipeline metadata only
no budget scheduler or allocator was run
no review-capacity queue optimizer was run
no production scheduler log or live workload-quality review was recorded
no load-stability or verification-tax experiment was run
no KV-cache, serving, or economic result is established
no simulation run, fidelity calibration, or transfer-validity review was run
no physical-computation audit or physical constraint measurement is established
downgrade_triggers: resource CI cost profile validation failure
chapter text interpreted as measured budget optimization
scarce review-capacity fixture interpreted as deployed queue behavior
workflow trace interpreted as production scheduler behavior
local command replay interpreted as live workload-quality evidence
local workload-quality probe interpreted as stable speedup, external review, or production workload-quality evidence
CI publication metadata interpreted as production workload evidence
KV-cache or serving context treated as reproduced local performance evidence
simulation_contract_record fixture interpreted as a simulator result
SimulationFidelity record predicates interpreted as physical feasibility or transfer evidence
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to deterministic accounting, workflow-trace, toy-trace, or local route-selection behavior that passed, or after budget-allocation runs, production scheduler logs, externally reviewable workload-quality reviews, review-capacity allocation traces, real load-stability scenarios, cost-quality-residual measurements, serving-memory or KV-cache measurements, verification-tax experiments, and independent source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records. The local five-sample workload-quality probe can support only a narrowed repository-task route-selection claim unless live or externally reviewed evidence justifies more. Folded simulation-fidelity claims also require simulation-transfer reviews, physical-computation audits, fidelity calibration, benchmark-transfer negative cases, measured simulation outputs, and accepted evidence-transition records before any stronger resource or simulation claim is promoted.
limitations: deterministic fixtures do not implement a scheduler or allocator
scarce review-capacity fixtures do not optimize or measure a real review queue
capacity-smoothing fixtures are toy traces and do not measure real load stability
workflow traces are deterministic fixtures and do not measure production scheduler behavior
local command replay measures repository validation replay only, not workload quality
local workload-quality probing measures five samples for one repository validation route choice only, not stable speedup or external workload quality
CI cost profiles measure publication-pipeline metadata only, not deployed resource economics
schema validation does not prove load stability or budget optimality
external serving literature supplies comparison vocabulary rather than reproduced local measurements
verification tax remains a required accounting field, not an optimized result
no independent economic, serving, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
simulation-contract schema validation does not prove simulator adequacy, physical feasibility, or transfer
finite SimulationFidelity predicates prove record-level gates only, not external physics assumptions or open-world deployment relevance
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent scheduler, serving-system, economic, benchmarking, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no external or live workload-facing quality review beyond the local repository task
no production scheduler log beyond CI publication metadata
no simulation run or transfer-validity review
no physical-computation audit or fidelity calibration
non_claims: does not promote Resource Economics above argument
does not prove production scheduler behavior
does not prove live workload quality
does not prove stable speedup or external workload-quality review
does not prove KV-cache or serving performance
does not prove verification-tax optimization or economic outcomes
does not prove simulation results transfer
does not prove physical feasibility or simulator adequacy
